title: unable to pass options to slirp4netns
body:
```
podman pod create --network slirp4netns:enable_ipv6=true ...
```

results in the following error: 

```
error configuring network namespace for container 8141426fb804b275465f49cb30009790ee07febb65336ce9c5ea4bf4006608e4: CNI network "slirp4netns:enable_ipv6=true" not found
```

The Manpage says: `slirp4netns[:OPTIONS,...]: use slirp4netns to create a user network stack.` so it should be a valid option. 

podman version 2.1.1

comments.body: This is only supported since v2.2
Ah thats interesting, I now have to understand why Coreos fc33 and Fedora fc33 are shipping different Versions. Thanks ;-)
